title: Hot Water Funnel — Sales-Led Strategy
description: Sales-led strategy to own Gold Coast heat pump hot water demand through fixed-price positioning, Google Ads, local SEO, Google Business Profile, and a 90-day conversion system.
tldr: A fixed $3,600 standard-install offer gives the business a clearer sales message than broader plumbing competitors.
Estimated Gold Coast search demand sits around 1.5k–3k+ monthly across heat pump, replacement, rebate, and brand-intent searches.
The first 90 days should launch one connected system across Google Ads, local SEO, Google Business Profile, conversion pages, eligibility, booking, and deposits.
A specialist market position can outperform general local operators because the offer is easier to remember, trust, and choose.
The recommended package is the Full-Funnel Revenue Engine at $14,750 over 90 days, with full site coverage, daily GBP activity, ads, SEO, automation, and reporting.
At $3,600 per standard install, even 2–4 booked installs per week can generate $28.8k–$57.6k in monthly gross revenue before scaling higher.
The rollout should progress month by month: core pages and campaigns first, then broader local coverage, then compounding reviews, referrals, and optimisation.
Keep the public promise simple for standard installs and route non-standard jobs into manual review before final confirmation.

hero.eyebrow: Gold Coast heat pump hot water strategy
hero.headline: Own Gold Coast heat pump demand
hero.subtitle: Turn local search, Google Ads, and Google Business Profile into booked installs with one specialist conversion system.

executiveSummary.heading: Launch a specialist local acquisition system that starts producing qualified installs in the first 90 days, then use those wins to keep strengthening the channel month by month.
executiveSummary.subtitle: You are not buying a brochure site. You are launching a revenue channel that combines Google Ads, local SEO, Google Business Profile, conversion-led pages, an eligibility path, and ongoing optimisation around one clear offer.

metrics.label: Standard install offer
metrics.note: A clear fixed-price offer gives your business a cleaner sales message than generic quote-first competitors.

metrics.label: Gold Coast demand
metrics.note: Estimated monthly search demand across heat pump, hot water replacement, rebate, and brand-intent terms.

metrics.label: Launch period
metrics.note: The first 90 days put the channel live fast enough to start producing jobs and collecting real closed-job feedback.

metrics.label: Target output
metrics.note: Long-range weekly booked-install target once the system is live, refined, and reinforced by ongoing monthly work.

immediateActions.title: You get a specialist local revenue channel
immediateActions.detail: Your offer becomes a dedicated local acquisition system instead of another service page buried inside a generic plumbing website.

immediateActions.title: Your first 90 days focus on traction
immediateActions.detail: The opening phase gets the channel live, generates qualified demand, and aims to produce the first closed jobs quickly enough to create real commercial momentum.

immediateActions.title: Closed installs fund faster growth
immediateActions.detail: Once installs start closing, that revenue can help fund the next layer of SEO, backlinks, local authority, and funnel optimisation rather than forcing everything into one large upfront build.

sections.heading: Why Your Offer Can Win on the Gold Coast

sectionLead.takeaway: Your offer gives you a strong local angle: fixed price, strong savings story, and a buying decision that often arrives with urgency.
sectionLead.body: Buyers already understand the category, the price can be positioned credibly, and the path from replacement pain to booked install is cleaner than in many other local service categories.

stackCards.title: The buyer already understands the offer
stackCards.content: Heat pump hot water has a cleaner buying story than many local trades offers: lower running costs, better efficiency, and a replacement decision that often arrives with urgency.

stackCards.title: The pricing can be positioned credibly
stackCards.content: Broader Australian installation benchmarks still leave room for a $3,600 standard-install offer to feel commercially believable rather than confusingly cheap or over-engineered.

stackCards.title: A specialist brand can out-position generalists
stackCards.content: Most local competitors still present as broad plumbing or multi-trade businesses. A narrower specialist position makes your business easier to remember, easier to trust, and easier to choose.

stackCards.title: The Gold Coast supports the product story
stackCards.content: Local climate conditions and household economics make the efficiency and savings story easier to communicate than in colder, more technically awkward markets.

sections.heading: What We Will Launch for You

sectionLead.takeaway: We will launch one connected system across acquisition, local visibility, qualification, booking, and monthly optimisation.
sectionLead.body: Google Ads captures demand. On-page SEO builds the service and suburb footprint. Off-page SEO builds authority through citations, backlinks, niche edits, guest posts, and listicles. Google Business Profile and the funnel turn that visibility into booked installs.

stackCards.title: Google Ads management
stackCards.content: We will set up and manage a paid acquisition layer that captures high-intent demand around replacement, rebate, heat pump, and brand-led searches.

stackCards.title: Website and landing pages
stackCards.content: We will build a conversion-led site structure around one clear offer, one clear CTA path, and pages that support both paid traffic and organic search visibility.

stackCards.title: Google Business Profile and local SEO
stackCards.content: We will build the local visibility layer through Google Business Profile optimisation, on-page local SEO, service-area relevance, and supporting trust signals.

stackCards.title: Eligibility, booking, and deposit flow
stackCards.content: We will reduce quote friction by moving weaker-fit leads out early and guiding stronger-fit leads into a clearer qualification, booking, and deposit path.

stackCards.title: On-page and off-page SEO
stackCards.content: We will grow both the on-page SEO footprint and the off-page authority layer through service and suburb content, citations, backlinks, niche edits, guest posts, listicles, and ongoing local optimisation.
bullets: Service, suburb, and service + suburb page expansion
Citations, backlinks, niche edits, guest posts, and listicle placements
Ongoing on-page SEO, off-page SEO, GBP, and funnel improvement

sectionLead.takeaway: The Full-Funnel Revenue Engine is the complete build. The other two options exist as reduced-scope downsells, not equal alternatives.
sectionLead.body: Each tier steps down the local footprint, ad coverage, content depth, automation, and GBP cadence so the top package remains the obvious commercial choice if the goal is to own the niche properly.

stackCards.label: Recommended
stackCards.title: Full-Funnel Revenue Engine
stackCards.content: This is the complete build: full on-page SEO footprint, full off-page authority building, full paid search coverage, full funnel automation, full Google Business Profile cadence, and the full customer journey from click to deposit to review to referral.
bullets: 1 homepage anchored around the $3,600 standard-install offer
240–320 service + suburb pages across the 4 core services
FAQ, about, reviews, refer, eligibility, privacy policy, and terms & conditions pages
citation building and local directory placements
niche edits and contextual link placements
guest posts, listicles, and comparison placements
brand mention reinforcement around core service and suburb targets
4 campaign-aligned landing pages, one per ad set
4 search campaign clusters: high intent, brand, rebate / savings, replacement / upgrade
ad copy, keyword sets, negatives, assets, geo targeting, and conversion tracking
weekly optimisation against lead quality and deposit conversion
full GBP setup across categories, services, products, Q&A, and photos
GBP offer setup and CTA alignment to the eligibility funnel
1 GBP post per day for 90 days
review request framework and review response protocol mirroring the source plan
booking calendar and Stripe deposit flow
ServiceM8 job creation flow and internal notifications
confirmation email + SMS, 48-hour reminder, on-the-way SMS, Day 3 check-in, Day 7 review email, Day 14 referral email, and Day 30 follow-up email
schema, metadata, canonicals, sitemap, and GSC-ready structure
call, form, quiz, booking, and deposit tracking
monthly reporting against acquisition, funnel, review, referral, and revenue KPIs

stackCards.label: Reduced scope
stackCards.title: Growth Launch
stackCards.content: This keeps the core acquisition and conversion system intact, but cuts back the on-page footprint, off-page authority activity, daily local activity, and automation depth. It still launches properly, just without the full local domination buildout.
bullets: FAQ, about, reviews, eligibility, privacy policy, and terms & conditions pages
foundational citations and local directory placements
niche edits and contextual authority placements
guest posts, listicles, and external mention work on priority targets
ad copy, keyword sets, negatives, and conversion tracking
weekly optimisation on the live campaigns
GBP offer setup and CTA alignment
booking request flow and deposit flow
Day 3 check-in and Day 7 review request
What drops off vs the Revenue Engine
lighter off-page SEO outreach and authority reinforcement
no referral page or referral automation

stackCards.label: Entry tier
stackCards.title: Lean Launch
stackCards.content: This is the stripped-down downsell: enough to test demand, launch the core page set, and establish a light off-page foundation, but nowhere near the full footprint needed to own the market.
bullets: FAQ, eligibility / enquiry, privacy policy, and terms & conditions pages
basic backlink and authority setup around the main service page
basic keyword targeting and conversion tracking
GBP offer setup and CTA alignment
simple eligibility capture and manual booking handoff
What drops off vs the Revenue Engine
no service + suburb matrix at scale
no review or referral automation stack

sectionLead.takeaway: If the goal is to build the full engine properly, this is the 90-day commercial model to anchor on.
sectionLead.body: These figures apply to the recommended package only: full website footprint, full ad coverage, daily GBP cadence, and the complete funnel / automation stack.

stackCards.label: Month 1
stackCards.content: Month 1 includes setup / launch plus the first month of ads management, SEO + authority, and paid media for the Full-Funnel Revenue Engine.

stackCards.label: Month 2
stackCards.content: Once the channel is live, the spend shifts fully into management, SEO, and media rather than additional setup cost.

stackCards.label: Month 3
stackCards.content: Month 3 keeps the full package running long enough to produce real optimisation data and the first meaningful closed-job cycle.

stackCards.label: Full-Funnel total
stackCards.content: This is the 90-day investment for the recommended package — the only tier that includes the full website footprint, daily GBP cadence, and complete automation stack.
bullets: first closed jobs begin funding the next layer of work

sectionLead.takeaway: At $3,600 per standard install, the economics become meaningful quickly once the funnel starts producing booked jobs consistently.
sectionLead.body: The ranges below show what booked-install volume can look like in gross revenue terms as the channel moves from early traction into stronger weekly output.

stackCards.label: Conservative traction
stackCards.title: 2–4 booked installs / week
stackCards.content:
$28.8k–$57.6k monthly gross revenue
Even at a conservative weekly pace, the economics become meaningful quickly because the offer value per standard install is already strong.

stackCards.label: Core performance range
stackCards.title: 4–8 booked installs / week
stackCards.content:
$57.6k–$115.2k monthly gross revenue
This is where the channel starts to look like a serious acquisition engine rather than a light experiment.

stackCards.label: Long-range target
stackCards.title: 8–15 booked installs / week
stackCards.content:
$115.2k–$216k monthly gross revenue
This is the longer-range upside once the funnel, local authority, and monthly optimisation are working together at a high level.

sections.heading: What Goes Live Across The First 90 Days

sectionLead.takeaway: The recommended package is not vague. Each month has exact outputs tied to the website, Google Ads, Google Business Profile, and the funnel stack.
sectionLead.body: This is the rollout for the Full-Funnel Revenue Engine — the package built to own the niche rather than just test it.

stackCards.label: Month 1
stackCards.title: Go live with the full acquisition system
stackCards.content: The first month gets the paid channel, core website, local presence, and booking flow live so the business can start taking qualified enquiries and deposits.
bullets: homepage, about, FAQ, reviews, eligibility, privacy policy, and terms & conditions live
4 core service pages and 4 paid landing pages live
first 30 suburb pages and first 120 service + suburb pages live
citation, directory, and foundational off-page SEO setup underway
Google Ads campaigns live with call, form, quiz, booking, and deposit tracking
GBP fully optimised and posting daily
booking calendar, Stripe deposit flow, and ServiceM8 connection tested end-to-end
What is live: By the end of Month 1, paid traffic can hit campaign-specific pages, organic traffic has real local coverage, and deposits can be taken through a working funnel.

stackCards.label: Month 2
stackCards.title: Expand the local footprint and tighten conversion
stackCards.content: The second month pushes the local SEO footprint forward, improves lead quality, and turns live search-term and conversion data into better pages and better ad performance.
bullets: brand pages and resource pages live
backlinks, citations, niche edits, guest posts, and listicle placements underway
review request flow running from completed installs
daily GBP posting continues and offer / CTA alignment stays tight
ads refined from live keyword, search-term, and lead-quality data
What is live: By the end of Month 2, the site looks materially deeper, the local footprint is much harder to ignore, and the funnel is qualifying traffic more effectively.

stackCards.label: Month 3
stackCards.title: Finish the footprint and lock the growth loop
stackCards.content: The third month completes the first major build cycle and puts the compounding layer in place so the next quarter can scale from real commercial evidence.
bullets: off-page SEO compounding through backlinks, citations, guest posts, niche edits, and listicle reinforcement
referral page and referral email flow live on the Full-Funnel package
reporting, optimisation, and next-quarter scale plan in place
What is live: By the end of Month 3, the business has a full local acquisition system, a much larger SEO footprint, a live review engine, and a cleaner path to scale.

sections.heading: How Revenue Keeps Strengthening the Channel

sectionLead.takeaway: The first wins should strengthen the next round of SEO, authority building, and funnel optimisation.
sectionLead.body: Keep the monthly structure simple and use closed-job revenue to support the next layer of growth.

stackCards.title: Launch first, then scale with evidence
stackCards.content: Launch quickly, learn from real demand, and tighten the channel around actual closed jobs rather than assumptions.

stackCards.title: Closed jobs fund the next phase of growth
stackCards.content: Once installs start closing, that revenue can be used to fund more SEO coverage, more backlinks, more local authority work, and more funnel optimisation.

stackCards.title: The monthly structure stays simple
stackCards.content: After the first 90 days, the structure remains easy to understand: $1,000 / month for ads management, $2,000 / month for SEO + authority, plus the media budget that the closed-job economics can support.

sectionLead.takeaway: The offer works best when the public message stays simple and the operational boundaries stay explicit.
sectionLead.body: The fixed price should remain visible, but it needs a clear standard-install definition so unusual jobs route into review rather than creating delivery friction later.

bullets: no major relocation of the unit
no crane work or unusual access constraints

highlightBoxes.title: Commercial rule
highlightBoxes.body: Keep the public promise simple: $3,600 fully installed for standard installs. If the job requires relocation, switchboard work, unusual access, structural modification, or any other non-standard element, it should move into manual review before final confirmation.

cta.headline: Launch the full revenue engine, not a cut-down version
cta.body: If the goal is to own the Gold Coast heat pump niche properly, the Full-Funnel Revenue Engine is the cleanest option: full website footprint, full paid search coverage, daily GBP activity, full automation, and a 90-day rollout built to start generating installs and compound from there.
